title: A resolution supporting the goals and ideals of Korean American Day.
plain_summary: This resolution celebrates Korean American Day on January 13th every year. It honors the 122nd anniversary of when Korean immigrants first came to the United States. The resolution recognizes that Korean Americans have worked hard to overcome challenges and have helped make America better. Korean Americans have made important contributions in business, government, the military, and their communities. The resolution encourages all Americans to celebrate Korean American Day.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Resolution Supporting the Goals and Ideals of Korean American Day
sections.text: This resolution recognizes the important contributions Korean Americans have made to the United States. Korean Americans can be found in all parts of American life, including politics, business, volunteering, arts, and education. On January thirteenth, nineteen oh three, one hundred and two brave Korean immigrants came to the United States. This was the start of the first big wave of Korean people moving to America. These first Korean immigrants faced many hard challenges including social and economic problems and trouble with the English language. They came to America looking for the American dream and better opportunities. At first, Korean immigrants worked as farm workers, wage workers, and railroad workers across the United States. The first generation of Korean immigrants built new homes in this new country through their strength, hard work, and great sacrifices. Their efforts became the foundation for their children and future generations of Korean Americans. The year two thousand and three marked one hundred years since Korean immigration began, which was an important milestone. In December two thousand and five, both the House of Representatives and the Senate passed resolutions to honor Korean American Day. Korean Americans, like other immigrant groups who came to the United States seeking better lives and opportunities, have succeeded in their new country because of their strong work habits, close family relationships, and community spirit. Korean Americans have made important contributions to the economic strength of the United States and the world economy. Korean Americans have made history by winning elections at local, state, and federal levels of government. Korean Americans have strengthened businesses, non-profit organizations, government agencies, technology companies, medical facilities, sports, arts and entertainment, journalism, churches, schools, and many other areas of American life. Korean Americans have helped build and strengthen the friendship between the United States and South Korea, helping to promote peace on the Korean Peninsula. Korean Americans have made huge contributions to the military strength of the United States and have served with honor in the Armed Forces during World War One, World War Two, the Vietnam War, the Korean War, and other military conflicts around the world. The Centennial Committees of Korean Immigration and Korean Americans have chosen January thirteenth of each year as Korean American Day to remember the first step of the long and successful journey of Korean Americans in the United States.

sections.title: Senate Resolution
sections.text: The Senate supports the goals and ideals of Korean American Day. The Senate encourages all people in the United States to celebrate Korean American Day so they can better understand and appreciate the valuable contributions Korean Americans have made to the United States. The Senate honors and recognizes the one hundred and twenty second anniversary of when the first Korean immigrants arrived in the United States.
